what restrictions would the Act impose on you following separation from your current

assist private-sector companies that conduct business with ihe State of Califomia?

references are to the Government code" unless otherwise indicated. the regulations of the Fair political practices

CONCLUSION
We review in general terms the Act's limitations applicable to your post-retirement
employment plans, and further advise that the Act also resiricts yo,,. gou..n'rr,ental decision-
making powers in matters directly relating to any person with wirom lou.rruy presently be
negotiating, or have any arrangement concerning, prospective employment. '
FACTS
You are the Director of the Offl"" of Technology Services ("OTech,,), a state agency
formerly known as the Department of Technology seriices ("DTS"). The latter was created by
statute in 1995 following consolidation of the Teile Data Center, the Health and Human Services
Data Center, and the office of Network Service (a part of the Department of General Services).
oTech/DTS is a typical govemment data center providing computing services to state
and local govemments on a fee-for-service basis. As Diiector, yoo do not hive responsibility for
the approval of these services or their budgets. Your department resides within the cabinet-level
office of the CIo, which has approval and oversight responsibility for state IT projects.
You were appointed to your position by the Govemor, and as the Govemor,s term comes
to an end you will be leaving state service. You are considering two possible non-governmental
employment options; employment with a company that providei technology services to
govemments in Califomia and elsewhere, or establishing a consulting praJtice of your own.
The focus of your consulting practice would be working-with privatelsector companies that do
business with the state. specifically, you would provid! the following serrices:
o Strategic planning;
o Development of marketing and business plans;
o Assisting in the development of proposals responsive to bid requests;
o Helping companies identify potential contract opportuniti", *i product positioning;
o Business development services;
o Training on State administrative processes and requirements.
A1\ALYSIS
Public officials are subject to three types of post-governmental employment restrictions
under the Act. A one-year "revolving door'i iestriction b-ars certain rtut" e,lrpioyees from
communicating, for compensation, with their former agencies for the purpo* oi influencing
certain administrative or legislative action. (Section tl qoO,Regulation t1l a6.i .l
A permanent ban prohibits a former state employee from "switching sides,, to participate,
for compensation, in a proceeding involving the state oicalifornia if the ploceeding is one in
which the former stale_employee participated while employed by the ,,o,". 1s."tions g7401-
87 402, Regulation 187 41. l.)

Finally, there is a restriction on influenclng prospective employment that prohibits a
public employee from participating in, or using his or her official position to influence, a
govemmental decision directly relating to any person with whom Le or she is negotiating, or has
an arrangement concerning, prospective employment. (Section BT4oT,Regulation 1s747.)
The One-Year .,Revolving Door, Restriction
section 87406 prohibits specified officials from acting as agents or attomeys or otherwise
representing, for compensation, "any other person, by makirg *yio*4 o. irrro.*at

issuance' amendment, awardtng, or rev-ocation o/ o pL*it, license, grant, or contract, or the
sale or purchase of goods or property.,, (Empha;is;dd;.)
Regulation lB7 46.2(a) further provides:
"(a) For purposes of Government Code Section B74o6,a formal or informal
appearance or oral or written communication is for the purpose oiirnurn"ing if it
is made for the principal purpose of supporting, promotirg, innu"r.irg,

"statement of Economic Interests" as a,"desigla;d .rnptoy."" under the agency,s conflict of
interest code, and you are therefore subject tJthe a.i;r-l#-y"ar ban.
Regulation 18746'l(b) outlines the circumstances when the prohibitions of the one-year
ban will apply' Under this regulation, an official covered by the one-year ban is prohibited from
making an appearance or communication if alr of the foilowing appry:
"(1) The official has left his or her state office or employment, which means he or
she has either permanently left state service or is on u l"ur" of absence.
"(2) The appearance or communication is made within 12 months after leaving
state office or employment.
"(3) The public official is compensated, or promised compensation, for the
appearance or communication. However, a payment made for r"""rrory travel,
meals, and accommodations received airectiyin connection with voluniary
services is not prohibited or limited by this section.

whether a particular meeting or conversation is for the purpose of influencing

Similarly' as noted above, Regulation 18746.1(b)(sXA) provides that performing services
to administer, implement, or fulfill th-e requirements oi an existing permit, Iicinse, grant,

Section 87406, so long as you do not influence oit ", uoministrative or legislative action, or the

Accordingly, for purposes of the one-year ban under Section g74o6,the Act does not

The Permanent Ban on,.Switching Sides,
This permanentbanprohibits a former state employee from..switching sides,, and
